5.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Overwhelmingly bacteriophage-dominated (>90% of classifiable reads in surface seawater); eukaryotic NCLDVs a small fraction, higher at depth.
5.expected_na_type: DNA-dominated (dsDNA tailed phages + ssDNA Microviridae); RNA low single digits.
5.plausibility_verdict: MATCH. phage 95.4% / DNA 97% squarely expected; Gong 2018 reports Caudovirales 67.7-72% of surface viruses, euk NCLDVs enriched at depth not surface. Top Peduoviridae is a P2-like Caudoviricetes phage family.

citations.title: Viral Diversity and Its Relationship With Environmental Factors at the Surface and Deep Sea of Prydz Bay, Antarctica.
citations.supports: Surface seawater viromes 79.7-94.1% dsDNA; Caudovirales most abundant in surface (67.7-72%); eukaryotic NCLDVs dominate only deep water.

citations.title: T4-like myovirus community shaped by dispersal and deterministic processes in the South China Sea.
citations.supports: Abundant tailed dsDNA (T4-like/Straboviridae-type) phage communities structure marine surface waters.

citations.title: Novel pelagiphage isolate Polarivirus skadi is a polar specialist that dominates SAR11-associated bacteriophage communities at high latitudes.

6.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Strongly bacteriophage-dominated; largest single category typically unknown/novel rather than eukaryotic virus.
6.plausibility_verdict: MATCH. phage 89.2% / DNA 89.3% with 10.2% unknown-host matches soil-virome reality (Santos-Medellin 2021, Ter Horst 2021: majority of vOTUs novel/unclassified). Top Straboviridae (T4-like) is a Caudoviricetes phage family.

citations.title: Viromes outperform total metagenomes in revealing the spatiotemporal patterns of agricultural soil viral communities.
citations.supports: Agricultural-soil dsDNA viral communities; 2684 of 2961 vOTUs exclusive to viromes; dsDNA phage-dominated with large novel fraction.

citations.title: Minnesota peat viromes reveal terrestrial and aquatic niche partitioning for local and global viral populations.
citations.supports: dsDNA soil viral communities (4326 vOTUs) with narrow (single-genus) predicted host ranges; bacteriophage dominance.

citations.title: Genetic and functional diversity of ubiquitous DNA viruses in selected Chinese agricultural soils.
citations.supports: Agricultural soils harbor ubiquitous diverse DNA viruses (bacteriophages).

7.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Strongly bacteriophage-dominated (Caudoviricetes + Microviridae); eukaryotic algal Phycodnaviridae a small fraction.
7.expected_na_type: DNA-dominated; a modest single-digit RNA share acceptable.
7.plausibility_verdict: MATCH. phage 99.2% / DNA 92.7% consistent with temperate freshwater lakes (Roux 2012; Aguirre de Carcer 2015). Top Autotranscriptaviridae is a T7-like Caudoviricetes phage family. Minor: euk 0.6% low (Phycodnaviridae may be underrepresented); RNA 7% slightly high but plausible.

citations.title: Assessing the diversity and specificity of two freshwater viral communities through metagenomics.
citations.supports: Temperate lakes; DNA phage-dominated with Caudovirales plus Microviridae/Circoviridae/Nanoviridae.

citations.title: Biodiversity and distribution of polar freshwater DNA viruses.
Extended Evaluation of Viral Diversity in Lake Baikal through Metagenomics.
citations.supports: Large freshwater lake viral community is phage-dominated.

9.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Strongly bacteriophage-dominated by richness and abundance (~60-95% of classifiable reads); enteric eukaryotic pathogens usually only ~1-2% of reads. ViroForge inverts this.
9.expected_na_type: DNA phages dominate abundance; RNA (enteric ssRNA+ plus plant RNA) a minority unless RNA-enriched; ~45% RNA is high for a combined DNA+RNA virome.
9.plausibility_verdict: MISMATCH - eukaryotic and RNA fractions over-weighted. ViroForge correctly INCLUDES enteric eukaryotic families but inverts the proportion (65% euk / 45% RNA vs expected phage-majority). Internal inconsistency: top family Microviridae is an ssDNA phage, yet phage totals only 35%. Rebalance to phage-majority/DNA-majority unless explicitly modeling an RNA-enriched enteric-surveillance protocol.

citations.title: Initial Mapping of the New York City Wastewater Virome.
citations.supports: 14 NYC WWTPs: phages the largest viral component; ~1,700 novel virus clusters per sample.

citations.title: Occurrences of similar viral diversity in campus wastewater and reclaimed water of a university dormitory.
Analysis of dsDNA and RNA viromes in methanogenic digesters reveals novel viral genetic diversity.
citations.supports: dsDNA viromes dominated by Caudovirales; RNA viruses less diverse, mostly plant-infecting; <2% putative human/animal pathogens.

citations.title: Characterisation of the sewage virome: comparison of NGS tools and occurrence of significant pathogens.
citations.supports: Untargeted urban sewage virome dominated by bacteriophages; human/vertebrate viruses a smaller component.

citations.title: Raw sewage harbors diverse viral populations.
citations.supports: Raw sewage extremely diverse and phage-rich; human viruses one of many minor categories; vast majority of reads unclassified.

14.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: For an arbovirus/RNA-focused mosquito virome: essentially 100% eukaryotic RNA viruses, no phage.
14.plausibility_verdict: MATCH. 100% eukaryotic / 100% RNA with Flaviviridae top is correct (Faizah 2020; Wang 2021; Mohamed Ali 2023). Nuance (not a defect): in real mosquito viromes insect-specific viruses usually dominate and true arboviruses are a minority; a Flaviviridae-top abstraction is reasonable for an arbovirus-focused design.

citations.title: Deciphering the Virome of Culex vishnui Subgroup Mosquitoes, the Major Vectors of Japanese Encephalitis, in Japan.
citations.supports: Arbovirus-vector mosquito virome composed of eukaryotic RNA viruses including Flaviviridae.

citations.title: Establishment of Culex modestus in Belgium and a Glance into the Virome of Belgian Mosquito Species.
citations.supports: Belgian mosquito viromes: >=33 eukaryotic viral species, predominantly RNA insect-specific viruses.

citations.title: Longitudinal Study of Viral Diversity Associated with Mosquito Species Circulating in Cambodia.
citations.supports: Mosquito-associated viral diversity dominated by eukaryotic RNA viruses.
